reason: The text fragment demonstrates personal initiative and action by Nehemiah, the governor, in addressing the issues faced by the people and taking steps to rectify the situation without merely following commands from Yahweh directly. Nehemiah consults with himself, rebukes the nobles and rulers, sets a great assembly against them, and takes an oath from the priests to restore lands and properties to the people. He also demonstrates personal sacrifice and commitment by not taking advantage of his position for personal gain. These actions show personal initiative and action in leadership.
